level_signals: Comp range $290K-$365K base, consistent with senior-level IC at Anthropic
Drives executive reporting and company-wide communications
Owns end-to-end program execution for high-stakes RSP commitments
Expected to make real-time technical trade-off decisions independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience driving cross-functional projects, building longer-running programs, and interfacing with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Have experience executing technical programs that require systems and engineering-level knowledge.
Have a deep interest in and/or a willingness to learn about cybersecurity or regulatory compliance.
Have experience leveraging LLMs to automate workflows, improve operational efficiency, and develop novel solutions for complex technical and organizational challenges.
Have experience reporting on complex programs through data-driven benchmarks, including writing SQL queries.
Have strong interpersonal skills that enable you to influence without authority, build cross-organizational support, cooperation and action around security initiatives, policies and procedures.
Have a track record for being able to successfully organize, implement and manage complex programs and projects.
Are used to working through trade-offs, balancing competing priorities, and having your mind changed.
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

scope.surface: Security programs spanning security engineering, infrastructure, compliance, RSP commitments, and cross-functional teams at Anthropic
scope.business_model: AI safety research company, internal security program management
primary_metrics_inferred: RSP security readiness milestones met on timeline
Executive visibility into blockers and risks
Operational efficiency gains from security programs
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end security program execution for RSP security readiness
Lead infrastructure security programs (cluster hardening, service boundary enforcement, security observability)
Coordinate complex multi-team deployments (endpoint hardening, egress reduction)
Make real-time technical trade-off decisions during fast-moving program cycles
Build program structure for emerging security domains (charters, roles, timelines, communication strategies, success metrics)
Partner with third-party partners on security aspects of infrastructure partnerships
Lead stakeholder communication and executive reporting

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($290K-$365K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with reasonable effort commitment
Clear program scope tied to concrete RSP security commitments
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Role has real executive visibility and strategic ownership
Explicit acknowledgment of ambiguity tolerance needed — signals realistic expectations
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Comp range is very wide ($75K spread), suggesting significant uncertainty about level or scope
Years of experience requirement is deliberately vague ('will correlate with internal job level') — seniority bar is unclear without recruiter context
Role spans a very broad set of domains (security engineering, compliance, infrastructure, policy, third-party partnerships) which may indicate a high-load position

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and executive-facing responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (inferred from HQ mention; role location not explicitly named beyond hybrid policy)
ambiguities: Years of experience not stated numerically — JD says 'years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position', making minimum years unknown.

All items under 'You may be a good fit if you:' are framed as conditional/descriptive rather than hard requirements, yet they represent the only stated qualifications. Treated as hard must-haves given no separate 'required' section exists and these are the only stated criteria.
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent from the JD; no items were explicitly labeled preferred or bonus.
